tryout_question_steps.title: Identify the inner function
tryout_question_steps.why: We choose u = x^2+1 because its derivative 2x already appears in the integrand.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: u-substitution is reversing the chain rule.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: A common mistake is choosing u = (x^2+1)^3.

tryout_question_steps.title: Compute du
tryout_question_steps.why: The derivative of x²+1 is 2x.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: du must match a factor in the integrand exactly.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Forgetting to differentiate the constant: d(x²+1) ≠ x dx.

tryout_question_steps.title: Rewrite in terms of u
tryout_question_steps.why: Replace (x²+1) with u and 2x dx with du.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: The integral becomes a simple power rule problem.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Leaving 2x in the integral after substituting.

tryout_question_steps.title: Apply power rule
tryout_question_steps.why: ∫u³ du = u⁴/4 by the power rule.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Power rule: raise exponent by 1, divide by new exponent.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Writing u³/3 instead of u⁴/4.

tryout_question_steps.title: Back-substitute
tryout_question_steps.why: Replace u back with x²+1.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Final answer must be in terms of x.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Forgetting to substitute back.

tryout_question_steps.title: Apply LIATE: choose u and dv
tryout_question_steps.why: LIATE: Algebraic (x) before Exponential. Pick x as u.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: We want du simpler than u: x → 1 is simpler.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Choosing u = e^x makes things worse.

tryout_question_steps.title: Compute du and v
tryout_question_steps.why: Differentiate u, integrate dv.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: ∫e^x dx = e^x is standard.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Remember: integrate dv, not differentiate.

tryout_question_steps.title: Apply IBP formula
tryout_question_steps.why: ∫u dv = uv − ∫v du.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: IBP trades a hard integral for an easier one.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Forgetting the minus sign.

tryout_question_steps.title: Evaluate
tryout_question_steps.why: ∫e^x dx = e^x, then factor out e^x.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Remaining integral is simpler.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Writing +e^x instead of −e^x.

tryout_question_steps.title: Use the half-angle identity
tryout_question_steps.why: Even powers need the power-reduction identity.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: cos(2x) = 1 − 2sin²x rearranged.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Confusing with cos²x = (1+cos2x)/2.

tryout_question_steps.title: Integrate term by term
tryout_question_steps.why: ∫(1/2)dx = x/2 and ∫(cos2x/2)dx = sin(2x)/4.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: After identity, integral is straightforward.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Forgetting to divide by 2 for cos(2x).

tryout_question_steps.title: Let u = inner expression
tryout_question_steps.why: Expression inside the square root is the natural choice.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Inner part of composite function is usually best for u.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Don't substitute u = √(x²−4).

tryout_question_steps.title: Compute du
tryout_question_steps.why: We need x dx to match the integrand.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Solving for x dx gives the exact piece.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Be careful: divide both sides by 2.

tryout_question_steps.title: Substitute
tryout_question_steps.why: √u = u^(1/2), and x dx = du/2.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Rewriting square root as power lets us use power rule.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Don't forget the factor of 1/2.

tryout_question_steps.title: Apply power rule
tryout_question_steps.why: 1/2 × 2/3 = 1/3, simplifying the coefficient.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Dividing by 1/2 by mistake instead of 3/2.

tryout_question_steps.title: Back-substitute
tryout_question_steps.why: Replace u with x²−4.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Answer must be in terms of x.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Forgetting the exponent 3/2.

tryout_question_steps.title: Choose u = ln x (LIATE)
tryout_question_steps.why: Logarithm comes before Algebraic in LIATE.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: ln x doesnt have a clean antiderivative alone.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Choosing u = x leads to harder ∫ln x dx.

tryout_question_steps.title: Find du and v
tryout_question_steps.why: Derivative of ln x is 1/x; antiderivative of x is x²/2.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: du = 1/x simplifies beautifully with v = x²/2.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Forgetting /2 when integrating x.

tryout_question_steps.title: Apply IBP
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Shows why u = ln x was correct.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Simplify x²/2 × 1/x = x/2 carefully.

tryout_question_steps.title: Integrate and simplify
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Factor: x²/2·ln x − x²/4 = (x²/4)(2ln x − 1).
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Writing −x²/2 instead of −x²/4.

tryout_question_steps.title: Split one sin factor
tryout_question_steps.why: For odd powers, peel off one sin x.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Peeled sin x becomes du when u = cos x.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Using half-angle identity — for even powers only.

tryout_question_steps.title: Pythagorean identity
tryout_question_steps.why: sin²x = 1 − cos²x.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Setting up for u = cos x.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Wrong direction identity.

tryout_question_steps.title: Let u = cos x
tryout_question_steps.why: sin x dx = −du.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Integral becomes polynomial in u.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Forgetting the negative sign.

tryout_question_steps.title: Integrate and back-substitute
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Distribute negative sign carefully.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Sign error: −(u − u³/3) = −u + u³/3.

tryout_question_steps.title: Choose substitution
tryout_question_steps.why: ln x appears in the numerator and its derivative 1/x is in the denominator.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: When a function and its derivative both appear, substitution is ideal.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Don't let the fraction confuse you.

tryout_question_steps.title: Find du
tryout_question_steps.why: dx/x = du exactly matches the integral.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: The 1/x dx piece IS du.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Confusing du = (1/x)dx with du = x dx.

tryout_question_steps.title: Rewrite integral
tryout_question_steps.why: ln x → u and dx/x → du.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Entire integral collapses to simplest form.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: None if done correctly.

tryout_question_steps.title: Integrate and back-substitute
tryout_question_steps.why: Power rule gives u²/2, then replace u = ln x.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Same as integrating x, but with u = ln x.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Writing ln(x²)/2 instead of (ln x)²/2.

tryout_question_steps.title: First IBP: u = x², dv = e^x dx
tryout_question_steps.why: x² is algebraic, e^x exponential — LIATE.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Each IBP lowers power of x by 1.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Don't choose u = e^x.

tryout_question_steps.title: First IBP result
tryout_question_steps.why: Apply ∫u dv = uv − ∫v du.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Remaining integral needs IBP again.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Forgetting the factor of 2.

tryout_question_steps.title: Second IBP on ∫2xe^x dx
tryout_question_steps.why: We know ∫xe^x dx = e^x(x−1). Multiply by 2.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Repeated IBP reduces polynomial powers.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Recomputing incorrectly.

tryout_question_steps.title: Combine results
tryout_question_steps.why: x²e^x − 2[xe^x − e^x] = e^x(x²−2x+2).
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Factor e^x for clean form.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Sign errors when distributing.

tryout_question_steps.title: Rewrite as sin/cos
tryout_question_steps.why: tan x = sin x / cos x — reveals f'/f pattern.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Look for numerator being derivative of denominator.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Trying to integrate tan x directly.

tryout_question_steps.title: Let u = cos x
tryout_question_steps.why: Transforms integral into −∫(1/u) du.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: sin x dx = −du.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Forgetting negative sign.

tryout_question_steps.title: Integrate and back-substitute
tryout_question_steps.why: −ln|cos x| = ln|1/cos x| = ln|sec x|.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Both forms are equivalent; ln|sec x| is conventional.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Writing ln|cos x| without negative.

tryout_question_steps.title: Spot the pair
tryout_question_steps.why: cos x is exactly the derivative of sin x.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Whenever sin^n(x) cos x appears, let u = sin x.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Don't choose u = cos x here.

tryout_question_steps.title: Substitute
tryout_question_steps.why: sin^5 x → u^5 and cos x dx → du.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Simple power rule problem.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Keeping sin x in the integral after substituting.

tryout_question_steps.title: Integrate and back-substitute
tryout_question_steps.why: Power rule: ∫u^5 du = u^6/6.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Clean power rule application.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Writing sin(x^6)/6 instead of (sin x)^6/6.

tryout_question_steps.title: Trick: write 1 as dv
tryout_question_steps.why: Can't directly integrate ln x, but can differentiate it.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Classic IBP trick — multiply by 1.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Trying to integrate ln x directly.

tryout_question_steps.title: du and v
tryout_question_steps.why: Differentiate ln x: 1/x. Integrate 1: x.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: v·du = 1 dx after cancellation.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: No common mistakes here.

tryout_question_steps.title: Apply IBP and simplify
tryout_question_steps.why: ∫v du = ∫x·(1/x) dx = ∫1 dx = x.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: x and 1/x cancel perfectly.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Writing +x instead of −x.

tryout_question_steps.title: Let u = tan x
tryout_question_steps.why: sec²x is the derivative of tan x.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Whenever sec²x appears next to tan x, let u = tan x.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: u = sec x also works — compare.

tryout_question_steps.title: Substitute and integrate
tryout_question_steps.why: Simple power rule.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Keep it clean.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: None.

tryout_question_steps.title: Back-substitute
tryout_question_steps.why: Replace u = tan x.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: sec²x/2 and tan²x/2 differ by a constant.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Both are correct answers.

tryout_question_steps.title: Let u = exponent
tryout_question_steps.why: Linear exponent makes substitution clean.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Unwrapping the chain rule.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Forgetting the factor of 3 in du.

tryout_question_steps.title: Rewrite
tryout_question_steps.why: dx = du/3, factor out 1/3.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: 1/3 compensates for the inner function speed.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Forgetting the 1/3 factor.

tryout_question_steps.title: Integrate and back-substitute
tryout_question_steps.why: ∫e^u du = e^u; back-substitute u = 3x.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: e^x integrates to itself.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Writing 3e^(3x) + C (that's the derivative).

tryout_question_steps.title: IBP #1: u = sin x, dv = e^x dx
tryout_question_steps.why: Neither simplifies — either can be u.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Requires two IBP applications (cyclic).
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Don't switch your u choice in second application.

tryout_question_steps.title: IBP #2: u = cos x, dv = e^x dx
tryout_question_steps.why: Apply IBP again to ∫e^x cos x dx.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Original integral reappears — cyclic IBP.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Keep same convention throughout.

tryout_question_steps.title: Algebraic solve
tryout_question_steps.why: Move repeated integral to left: I + I = 2I.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Treat I as unknown, solve algebraically.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Sign of recycled integral from minus outside bracket.

tryout_question_steps.title: Divide and add C
tryout_question_steps.why: Divide both sides by 2.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Factor 1/2 from cyclic structure.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Forgetting +C after dividing.

tryout_question_steps.title: Use half-angle identity
tryout_question_steps.why: cos(2x) = 2cos²x − 1 rearranged.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Parallel to sin²x but with + sign.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Mixing up signs: sin² has minus, cos² has plus.

tryout_question_steps.title: Integrate term by term
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Identical technique to sin²x except sign.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Writing −sin(2x)/4 (that is sin² case).

tryout_question_steps.title: Let u = denominator
tryout_question_steps.why: Numerator x dx is half of du.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: When numerator is derivative of denominator, use ln.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Not noticing x dx = du/2.

tryout_question_steps.title: Rewrite integral
tryout_question_steps.why: x dx = du/2, factor out 1/2.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Classic ln form: ∫(1/u) du.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Forgetting the 1/2 factor.

tryout_question_steps.title: Integrate
tryout_question_steps.why: ∫(1/u) du = ln|u|. Since x²+9 > 0, absolute value not needed.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: The ln integral is fundamental.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Writing ln(x²+9)/x instead of (1/2)ln(x²+9).

tryout_question_steps.title: Let u = sin x
tryout_question_steps.why: cos x is derivative of sin x.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Alternatively use sin x cos x = sin(2x)/2.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Both methods give equivalent answers.

tryout_question_steps.title: Integrate
tryout_question_steps.why: Power rule: u²/2; back-substitute u = sin x.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Simple and clean.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Don't confuse with ∫sin(x²) dx.

tryout_question_steps.title: Ingat rumus Integral by Parts
tryout_question_steps.why: Integral by Parts dipakai ketika integrand adalah hasil kali dua fungsi berbeda yang tidak bisa diselesaikan dengan u-substitusi. Di sini ada perkalian x dan eˣ.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Rumus ini berasal dari aturan perkalian (product rule) dalam diferensial. Bayangkan kita "memindahkan" diferensial dari satu fungsi ke fungsi lainnya.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Jangan gunakan u-substitusi untuk soal ini — tidak ada fungsi komposit di sini.

tryout_question_steps.title: Pilih u dan dv menggunakan LIATE
tryout_question_steps.content: \text{LIATE: Logaritma > Invers trig > Aljabar > Trig > Eksponensial}\\[6pt] u = x \quad (\text{Aljabar})\\[4pt] dv = e^x\,dx \quad (\text{Eksponensial})
tryout_question_steps.why: Aturan LIATE membantu kita memilih u: pilih fungsi yang lebih mudah diturunkan. Aljabar (x) lebih mudah diturunkan dari Eksponensial (eˣ), jadi u = x.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Trik LIATE: yang lebih kiri di daftar menjadi u, sisanya menjadi dv. x adalah Aljabar, eˣ adalah Eksponensial — Aljabar lebih kiri, jadi u = x.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Jika kita pilih u = eˣ dan dv = x dx, maka ∫v du malah lebih rumit. Pemilihan u dan dv yang salah membuat soal semakin susah.

tryout_question_steps.title: Hitung du dan v
tryout_question_steps.content: u = x \implies du = dx\\[6pt] dv = e^x\,dx \implies v = \int e^x\,dx = e^x
tryout_question_steps.why: Kita perlu empat bagian: u, v, du, dv. Turunkan u untuk dapat du, dan integralkan dv untuk dapat v.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Turunan dari x adalah 1, jadi du = dx. Integral dari eˣ adalah eˣ (fungsi ajaib yang integralnya sama dengan dirinya sendiri!).
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Jangan bingung antara du dan dv. du didapat dari menurunkan u, v didapat dari mengintegralkan dv.

tryout_question_steps.title: Terapkan rumus ∫u dv = uv − ∫v du
tryout_question_steps.content: \int x\,e^x\,dx = x \cdot e^x - \int e^x \cdot dx= xe^x - \int e^x\,dx
tryout_question_steps.why: Substitusikan u=x, v=eˣ, du=dx ke dalam rumus. Perhatikan: ∫v du = ∫eˣ dx (yang lebih mudah dari soal awal!)
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Keajaiban IBP: integral yang sulit (∫x·eˣ dx) diubah menjadi integral yang mudah (∫eˣ dx). Inilah tujuan memilih u dan dv dengan benar!
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Jangan lupa tanda minus! ∫u dv = uv MINUS ∫v du, bukan plus.

tryout_question_steps.title: Selesaikan integral yang tersisa
tryout_question_steps.content: xe^x - \int e^x\,dx = xe^x - e^x + C = (x-1)e^x + C
tryout_question_steps.why: ∫eˣ dx = eˣ + C (rumus dasar). Lalu faktorkan eˣ dari kedua suku untuk menyederhanakan jawaban.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Kita bisa faktorkan eˣ dari xe^x - e^x menjadi (x-1)eˣ. Bentuk ini lebih rapi tapi kedua bentuk (xe^x - e^x atau (x-1)eˣ) sama benarnya!
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Jangan lupa konstanta +C di akhir. Dan perhatikan tanda: xe^x − e^x, bukan xe^x + e^x.

tryout_question_steps.title: Choose u
tryout_question_steps.why: x² dx is 1/3 of du = 3x² dx.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Exponent x³ and derivative x² together signal u = x³.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Choosing u = x² makes it messier.

tryout_question_steps.title: Rewrite
tryout_question_steps.why: x² dx = du/3.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Factor out 1/3 for standard ∫e^u du.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Forgetting to factor out 1/3.

tryout_question_steps.title: Integrate and back-substitute
tryout_question_steps.why: ∫e^u du = e^u; replace u = x³.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Exponential integrates to itself.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Writing e^(x²)/3 + C.

tryout_question_steps.title: Ubah menjadi perkalian dua fungsi
tryout_question_steps.why: Integral ini terlihat hanya satu fungsi, tapi kita bisa tulis ln(x) = ln(x) × 1. Ini memungkinkan kita menggunakan Integral by Parts dengan mengambil "1" sebagai bagian dv.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Trik keren: kalau integrand hanya satu fungsi (seperti ln(x), arcsin(x), dll), kalikan dengan 1. Lalu ln(x) jadi u dan 1·dx jadi dv.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Banyak siswa tidak tahu cara mengintegralkan ln(x) dan langsung berhenti. Ingat: tulis sebagai ∫ln(x)·1 dx!

tryout_question_steps.title: Pilih u dan dv (LIATE)
tryout_question_steps.content: u = \ln(x) \quad (\text{Logaritma — paling kiri di LIATE})\\[6pt] dv = 1\,dx \quad (\text{sisanya})
tryout_question_steps.why: Logaritma berada di posisi pertama (L) dalam LIATE, jadi u = ln(x). Ini juga logis karena kita bisa dengan mudah menurunkan ln(x), tapi sulit mengintegralkannya langsung.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Kalau ada logaritma dalam integral, hampir selalu dia yang jadi u. Karena turunan logaritma (1/x) lebih sederhana dari logaritma itu sendiri.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Jangan pilih u = 1. Jika u = 1 maka du = 0 dan integral tidak terselesaikan.

tryout_question_steps.title: Hitung du dan v
tryout_question_steps.content: u = \ln(x) \implies du = \dfrac{1}{x}\,dx\\[6pt] dv = dx \implies v = \int 1\,dx = x
tryout_question_steps.why: Turunan ln(x) adalah 1/x (rumus dasar yang harus dihapal). Dan integral dari 1 dx adalah x.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Turunan ln(x) = 1/x adalah salah satu rumus paling penting! Ini juga menjelaskan kenapa ∫(1/x)dx = ln(x).
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Kesalahan umum: menulis turunan ln(x) sebagai 1 (lupa pembagi x). Yang benar: d/dx[ln(x)] = 1/x.

tryout_question_steps.title: Terapkan rumus IBP
tryout_question_steps.why: Substitusi u=ln(x), v=x, du=(1/x)dx ke rumus ∫u dv = uv − ∫v du. Perhatikan x × (1/x) = 1 saling menghilangkan — inilah keindahan IBP untuk ln(x)!
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Yang awalnya sulit (∫ln(x)dx) berubah menjadi mudah (∫1 dx = x). IBP mengubah masalah sulit menjadi sederhana.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Jangan lupa tanda minus. Dan perhatikan x × 1/x = 1 (bukan x²/x = x).

tryout_question_steps.title: Selesaikan dan sederhanakan
tryout_question_steps.why: ∫1 dx = x adalah integral paling dasar. Jangan lupa tanda minus di depannya!
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Hasil akhir x·ln(x) − x + C bisa juga ditulis x(ln(x)−1) + C. Keduanya ekuivalen.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Kesalahan paling umum: menulis x·ln(x) + x + C (tanda plus, bukan minus). Hati-hati dengan tanda dari rumus IBP.

tryout_question_steps.title: Kenali masalah: sin²(x) tidak bisa langsung diintegralkan
tryout_question_steps.why: Tidak ada rumus integral langsung untuk sin²(x). Kita perlu mengubah bentuknya menggunakan identitas trigonometri agar bisa diintegralkan.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Prinsipnya: ubah dulu ke bentuk yang kita bisa integralkan. Strategi ini sering dipakai di integral trigonometri.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Jangan langsung menulis ∫sin²(x) dx = (sin³x)/3. Rumus ∫xⁿ dx = xⁿ⁺¹/(n+1) hanya berlaku untuk variabel polinomial (x), bukan untuk fungsi trigonometri yang dipangkatkan!

tryout_question_steps.title: Gunakan identitas cosinus ganda
tryout_question_steps.why: Identitas cos(2x) = 1 − 2sin²(x) memungkinkan kita mengubah sin²(x) menjadi bentuk yang melibatkan cos(2x), yang bisa langsung diintegralkan.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Ingat identitas ini: cos(2x) = 1 − 2sin²(x). Kalau kita isolasi sin²(x), kita dapat sin²(x) = (1 − cos2x)/2. Ini "trik" utama soal ini!
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Hati-hati: ada dua versi identitas cos ganda — cos(2x) = 1−2sin²x (untuk sin) dan cos(2x) = 2cos²x−1 (untuk cos). Gunakan versi yang tepat sesuai soal.

tryout_question_steps.title: Substitusi identitas ke dalam integral
tryout_question_steps.why: Ganti sin²(x) dengan (1−cos2x)/2 yang sudah kita temukan. Faktor 1/2 bisa dikeluarkan dari integral.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Sekarang integralnya jauh lebih mudah: kita hanya perlu mengintegralkan 1 dan cos(2x) secara terpisah.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Jangan lupa faktor 1/2 di luar integral. Banyak yang langsung mengintegralkan tanpa memperhitungkan faktor ini.

tryout_question_steps.title: Integralkan suku per suku
tryout_question_steps.why: Kita pisahkan integral menjadi dua bagian. Untuk ∫cos(2x)dx, kita gunakan rumus yang sudah kita pelajari: hasilnya sin(2x)/2.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Integral dari cos(2x) mirip dengan Soal 1 (∫cos(2x)dx = sin(2x)/2). Jadi satu soal membantu soal lainnya!
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Untuk ∫cos(2x)dx, jangan lupa bagi dengan 2 (karena ada 2x di dalam). Hasilnya sin(2x)/2, bukan sin(2x).

tryout_question_steps.title: Gabungkan dan tuliskan jawaban akhir
tryout_question_steps.why: Gabungkan kedua hasil dan jangan lupa konstanta integrasi C. Distribusikan faktor 1/2 ke setiap suku.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Periksa dengan menurunkan jawaban: d/dx[x/2 − sin(2x)/4] = 1/2 − (2cos2x)/4 = 1/2 − cos(2x)/2 = (1−cos2x)/2 = sin²(x). ✓ Jawaban benar!
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Pastikan distribusi faktor 1/2 benar ke semua suku. 1/2 × sin(2x)/2 = sin(2x)/4, bukan sin(2x)/2.

tryout_question_steps.title: Kenali fungsi dalam (inner function)
tryout_question_steps.content: \text{Kita punya } \cos(2x). \text{ Bagian dalam kurung adalah } 2x.\\\text{Misalkan } u = 2x.
tryout_question_steps.why: Ketika ada fungsi komposit seperti cos(sesuatu), kita pilih "sesuatu" itu sebagai u. Di sini "sesuatu"-nya adalah 2x.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Bayangkan u-substitusi seperti mengganti nama — kita beri nama baru "u" untuk bagian yang bikin integral terlihat rumit, supaya lebih mudah dikerjakan.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Jangan pilih u = cos(2x) karena itu adalah fungsi luarnya, bukan dalamnya. Kita butuh u dari bagian dalam kurung.

tryout_question_steps.title: Hitung du (diferensial u)
tryout_question_steps.why: Kita perlu mengganti dx dengan sesuatu yang melibatkan du, supaya integral bisa ditulis sepenuhnya dalam variabel u.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Turunan dari 2x adalah 2, jadi du = 2 dx. Karena kita butuh dx saja, kita bagi dua ruas dengan 2: dx = du/2.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Kesalahan umum: lupa membagi dengan 2, sehingga menulis dx = du (salah!). Selalu selesaikan dx dari persamaan du.

tryout_question_steps.title: Ganti variabel dalam integral
tryout_question_steps.why: Setelah mengganti 2x → u dan dx → du/2, integral menjadi jauh lebih sederhana: integral dari cos(u) yang sudah kita hafal.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Nah, sekarang integralnya bersih! Tidak ada x lagi. Ini adalah bentuk dasar ∫cos(u) du yang hasilnya sin(u) + C.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Jangan lupa faktor 1/2 yang muncul dari dx = du/2. Kalau dilupakan, jawaban akhir akan dua kali lipat dari yang seharusnya.

tryout_question_steps.title: Integralkan ∫cos(u) du
tryout_question_steps.why: Rumus dasar: ∫cos(u) du = sin(u) + C. Ini adalah rumus yang harus dihafal. Faktor 1/2 tetap di depan karena dia bukan bagian dari integral.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Ingat ya: turunan dari sin adalah cos, jadi integral dari cos adalah sin. Arahnya berlawanan dengan turunan.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Jangan tulis ∫cos(u) du = -sin(u). Yang negatif adalah integral sin, bukan cos.

tryout_question_steps.title: Substitusi balik ke variabel x
tryout_question_steps.why: Jawaban akhir harus dalam variabel x (bukan u), karena soal awalnya dalam x. Ganti kembali u = 2x.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Kita hanya "membuka nama samaran" — u kita ganti kembali ke 2x.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Sering lupa melakukan substitusi balik dan meninggalkan jawaban dalam u. Jawaban dalam u tidak lengkap!

tryout_question_steps.title: Kenali pola f′(x)/f(x)
tryout_question_steps.content: \text{Perhatikan: pembilang } x \text{ hampir seperti turunan dari penyebut } x^2+1.\\[4pt] \text{Turunan dari } x^2+1 \text{ adalah } 2x.
tryout_question_steps.why: Ketika integral berbentuk f'(x)/f(x), hasilnya adalah ln|f(x)| + C. Di sini f(x) = x²+1 dan f'(x) = 2x. Pembilang kita adalah x, bukan 2x, tapi kita bisa menyesuaikannya.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Bayangkan: kalau kita punya angka/angka, dan angka di atas adalah "hampir" turunan dari bawah, maka u-sub adalah teknik yang tepat.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Jangan langsung mengintegralkan x/(x²+1) dengan cara biasa — itu tidak bisa langsung.

tryout_question_steps.title: Pilih u dan hitung du
tryout_question_steps.why: Kita pilih u = x²+1 (penyebut) karena turunannya, 2x, ada di pembilang. Walaupun tidak persis, kita bisa atur dengan membagi 2.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Trik: kalau pembilang adalah "setengah" dari turunan penyebut, kita tinggal tambahkan faktor 1/2 di depan.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Jangan pilih u = x (pembilang). Selalu pilih u dari fungsi yang "lebih rumit", yaitu penyebut.

tryout_question_steps.title: Tulis ulang integral dalam u
tryout_question_steps.why: Ganti x²+1 → u di penyebut, dan x dx → du/2. Hasilnya adalah ½ × integral dari 1/u, yang merupakan rumus dasar.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Integral dari 1/u adalah ln|u| — ini salah satu rumus paling penting dalam kalkulus!
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Pastikan semua variabel x sudah terganti dengan u. Tidak boleh ada x tersisa di integral.

tryout_question_steps.title: Hitung integral ∫(1/u) du
tryout_question_steps.why: Rumus dasar: ∫(1/u) du = ln|u| + C. Faktor ½ tetap di depan.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: Ingat ya: turunan dari ln(u) adalah 1/u. Jadi integral dari 1/u adalah ln(u). Ini kebalikan dari turunan!
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Jangan tulis ∫(1/u) du = u atau -1/u². Rumusnya adalah ln|u|, bukan pangkat negatif.

tryout_question_steps.title: Substitusi balik ke x
tryout_question_steps.why: Ganti u kembali ke x²+1. Karena x²+1 selalu positif (tidak pernah negatif), tanda nilai mutlak bisa dihilangkan.
tryout_question_steps.intuition: x²+1 ≥ 1 untuk semua nilai x, jadi nilainya selalu positif. Aman tanpa tanda |.|.
tryout_question_steps.mistakes: Jangan lupa substitusi balik! Dan perhatikan: kalau ekspresi dalam ln selalu positif, kita tidak perlu menulis tanda mutlak.
